states.content:
I'll search for report.pdf in your documents folder.

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/documents
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

I've initiated the search. Once I receive the results, I'll let you know what was found. If the file isn't found in the initial search, I can help you:
- Search in subdirectories
- Try different search patterns (in case of slight name variations)
- Search in other common locations

Let me know what the search returns and I'll proceed accordingly.

I see that no PDF files were found in /home/user/documents. Let me try a broader search to help locate report.pdf. I'll search for any files with "report" in the name first:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/documents
pattern: *report*
</tool_call>

This will help us find the file even if it has a different extension or slightly different name. Once I get these results, I can continue searching in other locations if needed.

It seems the search didn't find any files with "report" in the name either. Let me try searching in the parent directory to check other common locations:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

This will search more broadly across your user directory. If this doesn't find it, I can also check:
- Desktop
- Downloads folder
- Other common document locations

Let me know what this search returns and we'll keep looking until we find it.

I notice the tool results keep showing the same message about /home/user/documents even though I'm trying different search locations. Let me try searching in some other common locations:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/Downloads
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

I'm checking the Downloads folder now, as files are often saved there. If this doesn't find it, I can continue checking:
- Desktop (/home/user/Desktop)
- Root directory searches
- Alternative document folders

We'll keep searching until we locate the file or exhaust the likely locations.

I notice that the tool is consistently returning the same error message regardless of which path I specify in my search. It appears the filesystem_search tool may not be functioning correctly, as it keeps reporting "No files found matching *.pdf in /home/user/documents" even when I'm searching different locations with different patterns.

Unfortunately, without a properly functioning search tool, I'm unable to continue searching for report.pdf effectively. The issue seems to be with the tool itself rather than the file not existing.

To help you find your file, you might want to:
1. Check if there's an issue with the filesystem_search tool configuration
2. Try using your system's native file search (like `find` command on Linux/Mac or File Explorer search on Windows)
3. Check common locations manually: Downloads, Desktop, Documents subfolders

I apologize that I can't complete the search as intended due to this technical limitation.

description: Real LLM loop scenario on whatsapp
